tour.title: Engine Entry Point
tour.description: Everything starts in system4.c, which contains main(). It parses CLI options (--font-*, --audit, --skip-title), reads the game's System40.ini or AliceStart.ini config file to discover the .ain bytecode path, window dimensions, and mixer settings. It then loads the .ain file via libsys4, initializes the graphics/audio/input subsystems, applies per-game compatibility hacks, and finally calls vm_execute_ain() to begin bytecode execution. The xsystem4.h header defines the global config struct that all subsystems read from.

tour.title: The Virtual Machine Core
tour.description: vm.c is the heart of the engine at ~5000 lines. It implements a stack-based bytecode interpreter that executes AliceSoft .ain files. The main loop decodes opcodes and dispatches them: stack operations, arithmetic, control flow (JUMP/IFZ/CALLFUNC/RETURN), object lifecycle (NEW/DELETE), and crucially CALLHLL which bridges to native C code. It manages a 4096-frame call stack, handles v14 2-slot types (wrap/option/iface), delegate invocations, lambda closure propagation, and runs a periodic heartbeat that pumps SDL events and triggers GC. The vm.h header exposes the public API: vm_value union, stack manipulation, and function call/return interfaces.

tour.title: Memory Management: Heap and Pages
tour.description: The VM uses two tightly coupled memory systems. heap.c implements a slot-based, reference-counted heap using mmap virtual reservation for zero-copy growth. Each heap slot holds either a page or a string, tracked by generation counters for the mark-and-sweep cycle GC that detects circular references. page.c manages 'pages' -- ordered collections of typed variables used for globals, locals, struct instances, arrays (as multi-dimensional trees), and delegates. It includes a per-size free-list cache for fast allocation and handles v14 type initialization for wrap/inheritance. Together they form the VM's entire managed memory.

tour.title: FFI Bridge: Bytecode Calls Native Code
tour.description: ffi.c is the critical bridge between the AIN bytecode world and native C libraries, using libffi for argument marshalling. When the VM executes a CALLHLL opcode, it routes through this layer. ffi.c builds ffi_cif descriptors for each HLL function, handles all v14 type variants (AIN_WRAP, AIN_IFACE, AIN_OPTION, AIN_REF_*, AIN_HLL_FUNC callbacks), marshals arguments from the VM stack to C calling convention, and writes return values back. It also supports optional call tracing for debugging. This is the mechanism that lets .ain scripts call into engine subsystems like graphics, audio, and game logic.

tour.title: HLL Module System: Game API Bindings
tour.description: The HLL (Host-Link Library) system is how .ain bytecode accesses engine functionality. hll.h defines the DSL macros (HLL_EXPORT, HLL_LIBRARY, HLL_WARN_UNIMPLEMENTED) for declaring native function tables, plus inline helpers for v14 wrap parameter access. Each game's .ain file declares which HLL modules it needs; xsystem4 provides 80+ module implementations. SACT2.c is the foundational sprite/scene management module (sprite create/move/draw, audio, effects, input dispatch). SystemService.c provides core OS services (mixer, window, mouse, date/time). The system.c module handles v14-specific save/load (ResumeSave/ResumeLoad), event processing, and OS interaction.

tour.title: Graphics Initialization and Video Backend
tour.description: video.c creates the SDL window, initializes the OpenGL context, loads and links GLSL shader programs, and sets up the core VAO/VBO/IBO geometry. It handles viewport scaling with letterbox/pillarbox to preserve aspect ratio, manages framebuffer objects for off-screen rendering, and provides texture creation/deletion. On macOS it includes NSApplication activation and Sequoia SwiftUI workarounds. The gfx.h header defines the comprehensive 2D graphics API: texture lifecycle, shader management, and a library of blit/blend/fill/stretch/rotate operations. draw.c implements the actual GL shader-based 2D drawing primitives (color fill, alpha compositing, key-color, grayscale, rotation, dilation).

tour.title: Scene Graph and Sprite System
tour.description: scene.c is the compositor that maintains a Z-ordered list of sprites using a TAILQ. It manages sprite registration/unregistration, deferred sorting, wallpaper (WP) background textures, and the per-frame render loop that calls each sprite's render callback. sprite.c implements the sact_sprite type: texture/CG attachment, color and blend-rate modulation, draw methods (normal/screen/multiply/additive blending), text overlay rendering, hit-testing for mouse input, and draw_plugin binding that lets 3D engines attach to sprite surfaces. The scene renders every frame by iterating sprites in Z-order and calling their draw functions via OpenGL.

tour.title: Parts Engine: The UI Widget System
tour.description: The Parts Engine is a full UI component system for visual novel interfaces, spanning 10+ source files. parts.c is the core: it manages a global parts list and hash table, handles lifecycle (alloc/free/get/release), transform propagation (position/Z/alpha/scale/rotation/color), and the PE_Update main loop. construction.c implements deferred drawing pipelines where scripts queue operations to build textures. motion.c handles tween animation (position, alpha, CG frame, scale, rotation, vibration) with multiple easing curves. input.c processes mouse events to dispatch click events via the message queue. render.c draws each part type with full transform matrix, blend modes, and alpha-clipper support via GLSL shaders (parts.f.glsl/parts.v.glsl). PartsEngine.c is the HLL wrapper that AIN scripts call into.

tour.title: Audio System
tour.description: Audio is a three-layer stack. audio.c is the high-level interface for SACT2/KiwiSoundEngine: it manages WAV and BGM channel pools, one-shot sound playback, and the init/reset/update lifecycle. audio_mixer.c is the low-level mixer using the embedded sts_mixer library and an SDL audio device: it handles channel management, streaming decoding via libsndfile, volume/loop/fade control, and a hierarchical mixer tree for group volume. audio_meta.c loads BGI (BGM info) and WAI (WAV info) metadata files that describe track tables. KiwiSoundEngine.c is the v14 HLL module used by newer games like Dohna Dohna, providing a unified sound API with SE/BGM/voice channels.

tour.title: Text and Font Rendering
tour.description: text.c manages font loading and glyph rendering. It supports two backends: font_freetype.c (FreeType2 scalable fonts with bold embolden, kerning, SJIS metrics) and font_fnl.c (AliceSoft's pre-rendered FNL bitmap fonts). A hashtable glyph cache avoids redundant rasterization. The module provides both a stateless API (gfx_render_text for SACT2) and a stateful API (gfx_setfont_*/gfx_draw_text_to_* for DrawGraph). It handles SJIS/Unicode conversion, bold/edge/outline styles, and half-width character detection. The parts text subsystem (parts/text.c) builds on this to render per-character textures for UI text widgets.

tour.title: Screen Transitions and Effects
tour.description: effect.c manages 50+ named screen transition effects ranging from crossfades and blinds to TV-off simulations and page-turns. It dispatches to either GLSL shader pipelines or CPU blending routines. The GLSL effects (in shaders/effects/) take old and new scene textures plus a progress uniform (0 to 1) and compute per-pixel blending. Examples include blur_crossfade (Gaussian blur dissolve), mosaic (pixelation), turn_page (3D page curl), vwave_crossfade (sine-wave warp), and zigzag_crossfade (horizontal zigzag distortion). These effects are triggered by AIN scripts through the SACT2 TRANS (transition) API during scene changes.

tour.title: 3D Engine and SealEngine (Dohna Dohna)
tour.description: The 3D subsystem supports two engines. The Reign/Tapir engine (src/3d/) is used by titles like Toushin Toshi 3 and Rance Quest. renderer.c handles OpenGL shader loading, opaque/transparent draw passes, shadow mapping, outline rendering, and billboard particles. model.c parses AliceSoft's POL/MOT/AMT binary formats and uploads skinned meshes to GPU. reign.c manages the plugin lifecycle, skeletal animation with motion blending, and camera/lighting. SealEngine.c is the newer HLL wrapper used specifically by Dohna Dohna for 3D character rendering: it manages 3D instances, camera, bones, motions, and lighting through the same underlying renderer.

tour.title: ADV Engine and Key Game HLL Modules
tour.description: ADVEngine.c implements the visual novel dialogue scripting engine. It loads .adv script files, parses markup functions (text display, character sprites, waits, choices), and drives the step-by-step dialogue flow that players see. Array.c (85 functions, 2264 lines) is the largest HLL module, providing LINQ-style collection operations (Select, Where, Find, Sort, AddRange, Duplicate) for AIN generic arrays. Together with String.c (SJIS-aware search/split/replace/regex), Delegate.c (function pointer list management), and HashMap.c (string-to-int maps), these modules form the runtime library that complex games like Dohna Dohna depend on heavily.

tour.title: Save/Load and Resume System
tour.description: Save/load spans two subsystems. savedata.c handles AliceSoft's GSAVE/LSAVE format: it serializes per-global-group save files by traversing struct/array trees and writing binary or JSON output. resume.c handles mid-session state snapshots by converting the entire live heap and call stack to JSON, allowing the game to save and restore at any point during execution. The parts engine has its own serialization layer (parts/save.c) that writes the entire UI widget tree to an iarray page. The HLL-level save/load functions in system.c (ResumeSave/ResumeLoad, GroupSave/Load, SerializeStruct) are what .ain scripts actually call.

tour.title: Build System and Project Structure
tour.description: The root meson.build declares the project as C11 and resolves all dependencies: SDL2, FreeType2, libffi, libturbojpeg, libwebp, cglm, libsndfile, optional FFmpeg, and OpenGL (GLES or desktop GL+GLEW). It integrates the libsys4 subproject (the .ain file parser library declared in .gitmodules) and installs shaders/fonts/debugger.scm to the data directory. src/meson.build lists all C source files organized by subsystem and conditionally includes debugger sources, FFmpeg vs plmpeg movie backends, and FreeType vs FNL font backends. A CMakeLists.txt is also provided for Android builds targeting OpenGL ES. The Nix flake and Flatpak manifest enable reproducible builds on Linux.
